title: Malvastrum clandestinum
intro: Malvastrum clandestinum, also known as false mallow or wild mallow, is a plant species in the Malvaceae family, native to South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis. The bioactive constituents of Malvastrum clandestinum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Malvastrum clandestinum is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Malvastrum clandestinum belongs to the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. This family includes over 200 genera of flowering plants. The classification of Malvastrum clandestinum is further divided into the tribe Malveae, which comprises about 30 genera. As a member of the Malvaceae family, Malvastrum clandestinum is closely related to other plants such as hibiscus and okra.
